title: Podman doesn't implement identityToken (OAuth2) for registry credentials
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
When pulling from azure container registry, `docker pull` is successful whereas `podman pull` fails. Auth information is located under `~/.docker/config.json`. Podman documentation on `pull` specifies that if no `auth.json` file is found, then  `~/.docker/config.json` is used.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. az acr login

2. podman pull <images>

**Describe the results you received:**

 unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password

**Describe the results you expected:**

Pull successful

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.9
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[yihuaf@localhost km]$ podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.9
  podman version: 1.6.1
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.11.2
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.1-1.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.1, commit: 4346fbe0b2634b05857973bdf663598081240374'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  MemFree: 2509381632
  MemTotal: 16482402304
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-95.rc9.gitc1485a1.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc9+dev
      commit: 0840802d505e28d3adcbc7a275cbeadbdf64ddec
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 7904391168
  SwapTotal: 8308912128
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: localhost.localdomain
  kernel: 5.2.16-200.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    Package: slirp4netns-0.4.0-4.git19d199a.fc30.x86_64
    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.2
      commit: 19d199a6ca424fcf9516320a327cedad85cf4dfb
  uptime: 741h 40m 51.26s (Approximately 30.88 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: $HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-0.6.4-2.fc30.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.6.2
        fuse-overlayfs: version 0.6.4
        FUSE library version 3.6.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
  GraphRoot: $HOME/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 9
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
  VolumePath: $HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Local fedora 30 box.

comments.body:
Could you try --authfile=""
If you do a `podman login`, does everything work?
@QiWang19 I think removing the default from the options will fix this issue.

`--authfile=""` doesn't seem to work. Should I try converting .docker/config.json into a authfile? Is there a doc on the format of the authfile? I can't seem to find it.

Will try podman login a bit. Azure is a bit pain to use docker/podman login directly. Their CLI is hardcoded wrapper to docker login.

The authfile and the .docker/config.json file should have the same format.  I've created #4359 to find or fix that part of the doc up.  I would have expected to find that info on the podman-login/logout pages or at least a pointer to it from there.

If it's the same format, then `--authfile` is not being used at all, it seems. When pointing the authfile to `.docker/config.json`, from the log:

`DEBU[0000] Credentials not found`

@yihuaf Can you check your credentials exists in ~/.docker/config.json? I dailed to replica this issue with my plain text JSON format credential in ~/.docker/config.json.
Azure doc mentioned the token used by az acr login is valid for 1 hour, maybe it has been wiped out?

Then that doesn't explain why `docker pull` is successful. I also just tried with a fresh `az acr login` to make sure.

Here is the debug log if that helps.
```
[yihuaf@localhost ~]$ podman --log-level=debug pull kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo
DEBU[0000] using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"              
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /var/lib/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/lib/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /var/run/containers/storage   
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /var/lib/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /var/run/libpod                
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay" 
DEBU[0000] cached value indicated that overlay is supported 
DEBU[0000] cached value indicated that metacopy is being used 
DEBU[0000] cached value indicated that native-diff is not being used 
WARN[0000] Not using native diff for overlay, this may cause degraded performance for building images: kernel has CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS_REDIRECT_DIR enabled 
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=false, usingMetacopy=true 
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald          
DEBU[0000] using runtime "/usr/bin/runc"                
WARN[0000] Error initializing configured OCI runtime crun: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun: invalid argument 
INFO[0000] Found CNI network podman (type=bridge) at /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on]kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo" 
Trying to pull kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo...
DEBU[0000] reference rewritten from 'kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo' to 'kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo' 
DEBU[0000] Trying to pull "kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo" 
DEBU[0000] Credentials not found                        
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d for sigstore configuration 
DEBU[0000]  Using "default-docker" configuration        
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo 
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/kontainkubecr.azurecr.io 
DEBU[0000] GET https://kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/v2/     
DEBU[0000] Ping https://kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/v2/ status 401 
DEBU[0000] GET https://kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/oauth2/token?scope=repository%3Atest-node-fedora%3Apull&service=kontainkubecr.azurecr.io 
DEBU[0000] Error pulling image ref //kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo: Error initializing source docker://kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password 
  unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password
ERRO[0000] error pulling image "kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo": unable to pull kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo: unable to pull image: Error initializing source docker://kontainkubecr.azurecr.io/test-node-fedora:for_novdemo: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password 
```
Same log with adding `--authfile=""`.
content of docker config. removed the actual token
```
[yihuaf@localhost km]$ cat ~/.docker/config.json 
{
	"auths": {
		"kontainkubecr.azurecr.io": {
			"auth": "<removed>",
			"identitytoken": "<removed>"
		}
	},
	"HttpHeaders": {
		"User-Agent": "Docker-Client/19.03.4 (linux)"
	}
}
```

Could you try --authfile=~/.docker/config.json
To see if it reads it then?

It doesn't seem to read the file it all. It also doesn't complain if the file does not exist or is in bad format.

I even tried to re-install podman today, so it should be the latest:
```
[yihuaf@localhost km]$ podman version
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

az acr login made the ~/.docker/config.json different format (extra "identitytoken" field)  with podman auth.json
```
"qiwanredhat.azurecr.io": {
			"auth": ""
			"identitytoken": ""
}
```
Podman doesn't recgnize the login server in azurecr registry, trying to append docker.io before the server name.

```
podman pull qiwanredhat.azurecr.io/hello-world:v1
Trying to pull qiwanredhat.azurecr.io/hello-world:v1...
  unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password
Error: error pulling image "qiwanredhat.azurecr.io/hello-world:v1":: Error initializing source docker://qiwanredhat.azurecr.io/hello-world:v1: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password

```

Like: `docker.io.qiwanredhat.azurecr.io/hello-world:v1`? That doesn't seem to work.

Is identitytoken something specific to azurecr?
@yihuaf as an experiment, can you modify your `~/.docker/config.json` and put `docker.io` between the two `""` by the auth stanza and see if that works for you?

> Like: `docker.io.qiwanredhat.azurecr.io/hello-world:v1`? That doesn't seem to work.
> 
> Is identitytoken something specific to azurecr?

No.
I mean from the error mesage fof podman . Podman is trying `docker://qiwanredhat.azurecr.io` which doesn't work.
I haven't found a way to pull from azurecr.io successfully.
```
Error initializing source docker://qiwanredhat.azurecr.io/hello-world:v1: unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password
```

The `""` thing seems entirely unrelated to what @yihuaf has reported (the contents in https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4357#issuecomment-547628643 don’t contain that, at least).

The `identitytoken` field might be the actual cause, also mentioned in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/533 . Apparently that requires adding a (variant of?) OAuth2 support.

The issue https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/533 describes the same issue in fact. However, I don't see any issue in containers/image repo to track this. Should we file an issue in containers/image to get this resolved, perhaps referencing this issue and https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/533?
SGTM
@rhatdan https://github.com/containers/image/issues/748 filed. Thanks.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Bumped into this problem today as well

Doesn't resolved.
```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 03:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
Please try again with Podman 3.0.

@mheon well...
```
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "vfs" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve 
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "vfs" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve 
Error: Cannot connect to the Podman socket, make sure there is a Podman REST API service running.: vfs driver does not support overlay.mount_program options
```

@mheon Upgrade podman to 3.0.0 version and still `podman login` doesn't store any data in `~./docker/config.json`

UPD: Works with key `--authfile`
@rhatdan PTAL

Why would podman write to a file owned by Docker?  ~/docker/config.json. 
The podman man page says that podman will store data in:${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json.
for rootless containers.  Podman tools will use this file, and will fall back to use ~/docker/config.json if a user had done
`docker login` in the past.

```
man podman login
       podman  login logs into a specified registry server with the correct username and password. If the registry is not specified, the first registry under [registries.search] from registries.conf will be used. podman login reads in the username and password from
       STDIN.  The username and password can also be set using the username and password flags.  The path of the authentication file can be specified by the user by setting the authfile flag. The default path  for  reading  and  writing  credentials  is  ${XDG_RUN‐
       TIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json.  Podman will use existing credentials if the user does not pass in a username.  Podman will first search for the username and password in the ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json, if they are not valid, Podman will then use
       any existing credentials found in $HOME/.docker/config.json.  If those credentials are not present, Podman will create ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json (if the file does not exist) and will then store the username and password from STDIN as  a  base64
       encoded string in it.  For more details about format and configurations of the auth.json file, please refer to containers-auth.json(5)
```
My `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json` randomly disappears and I need to keep logging in, is that normal?

Everyone, please stop piling on a > 2-year-old report just because it has a _vaguely similar_ symptom.

Unless it is exactly the same thing, please file separate issues, with the requested information, and your steps to reproduce.

We can always close as duplicates, but mixing 3 concurrent conversations on 3 different topics on a single issue is impossible to untangle after-the-fact.

> My `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json` randomly disappears and I need to keep logging in, is that normal?

The XDG_RUNTIME_DIR directory may be put on tmpfs meaning it will disappear on reboot.
@fuomag9 https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12706
My `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` points to `/run/user/1000` which does indeed appear to be a tmpfs, shouldn't podman chose a different folder for saving credentials? e.g. `~/.config`
@fuomag9 see the referenced issue #12706
